Figure 22: Qualitative comparison using DVD, TRELLIS, and pipeline-native voxels

Figure 1: SLAT pipeline and uniform state discrete diffusion (USDM). We abuse the notation here to denote a whole voxel data as x0. Top: The two-stage SLAT pipeline. Stage 1 generates low-resolution sparse voxels. Stage 2 predicts a latent vector for each occupied voxel using flow matching with a sparse transformer (Sp. Flow) and decodes the latents (together with the scaffold) into downstream 3D representations via a sparse decoder. We substitute Stage 1 with discrete voxel diffusion. Bottom: Uniform state discrete diffusion. Reverse process: given a noisy sample xt, the model predicts the distribution over clean states q(x0|xt) (darker voxels indicate higher occupancy probability) and draws xs with ancestral sampling (refer to Section 3.1). For clarity, we visualize predictions only at occupied locations, although the model outputs q(x0 | xt) for all grid positions. Forward process: the forward process randomly reassigns each voxel to 0 or 1 with probability depend on t.
